evals.name: spend-on-groceries-last-month
evals.prompt: How much did I spend on groceries last month? Today is 2026-05-28.
evals.expected_output: A clear dollar figure for April 2026 spending in the Groceries category, formatted in dollars (not cents), with split transactions handled correctly (is_parent:false) so nothing is double-counted.

assertions.text: Answer shows a dollar amount formatted with $ and decimal cents (e.g. $123.45)
Answer identifies the time range as April 2026 (or 2026-04)
Answer does NOT report a raw-cents integer as if it were dollars (no 5+ digit number presented as the spend figure without a decimal)
Commands used at least one `actual` invocation that filters with `is_parent: false` to avoid double-counting splits
Commands resolved the Groceries category by ID or queried by `category.name` rather than guessing
Commands used a single aggregate AQL query (not a loop of one-per-day or one-per-tx queries)

evals.name: top-5-spending-categories-ytd
evals.prompt: What are my top 5 spending categories so far this year? Show me a table sorted by total spent, most first. Today is 2026-05-28.
evals.expected_output: A table showing 5 category names with totals in dollars (not cents), ranked by largest spend. Should aggregate via AQL groupBy on category.name with $sum on amount, filter to expenses only (amount < 0), is_parent:false, date range 2026-01-01 to 2026-05-28.

assertions.text: Answer is presented as a table (markdown table or similar tabular layout)
Answer shows 5 or fewer category rows
Amounts in the table are in dollars with decimals (not raw integer cents)
Table is ordered so the largest spend is first
Commands used a single AQL groupBy aggregate query (not 5+ separate per-category queries)
Commands filtered with `is_parent: false` to avoid double-counting splits
Date range used is 2026-01-01 through approximately 2026-05-28

evals.name: uncategorized-transactions-this-month
evals.prompt: List my uncategorized transactions from this month so I can clean them up. Today is 2026-05-28.
evals.expected_output: A list of transactions where category IS NULL (not the string 'Uncategorized') for the date range 2026-05-01 to 2026-05-28, with payee.name and amount visible in dollars, ordered by date. Should NOT include parent split rows.

assertions.text: Commands filter with `category: null` (the correct way to find uncategorized) — NOT by searching for a category named 'Uncategorized'
Date range filter is May 2026 (2026-05-01 onward)
Answer is a list or table the user can act on (shows date, payee, amount at minimum)
Amounts shown to the user are in dollars (with decimals), not raw integer cents
Commands filtered with `is_parent: false` (or otherwise avoided showing split parents)
